review_notes: The functions provided are generally applicable to the control AC-17(9) as they focus on restricting and controlling remote access to Kubernetes resources. The use of NetworkPolicies to deny ingress and egress by default is a valid approach to managing remote access. Restricting LoadBalancer services and ensuring TLS for Ingress resources are also appropriate measures. However, the function names could be slightly improved for clarity.
naming_improvements: Ensure that 'k8s_ingress_tls_enabled' clearly indicates that TLS is enforced for all ingress traffic.
title: Remote Access - Remote access | disconnect or disable access nist sp 800-53, r ev
step1_reasoning: The control AC-17(9) focuses on ensuring that remote access is properly managed and can be disconnected or disabled when necessary. In the context of Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that network access to the cluster and its services is restricted and controlled. NetworkPolicies can be used to deny ingress and egress by default, ensuring that only explicitly allowed traffic is permitted. Additionally, restricting the use of LoadBalancer services and ensuring TLS is enabled for Ingress resources helps prevent unauthorized remote access.
